# VaultPay — Test Strategy & Layer Assignment
<!-- Generated by /test-strategy skill | Input: scenarios.md (TC-001–TC-135) -->
<!-- Source verified against: src/App.jsx, business-rules.md, ui-selectors.md, user-flows.md -->

---

## 1. Test Pyramid Distribution Table

| Layer     | Count | % of Suite | Avg Run Time | Focus |
|-----------|-------|------------|--------------|-------|
| Unit      | 12    | 8.9%       | <5ms each    | Pure logic: validate(), passwordStrength(), filter predicate, Math.min cap |
| Component | 82    | 60.7%      | <100ms each  | Isolated render, state transitions, CSS class assertions, inline styles |
| E2E       | 41    | 30.4%      | 4–8s each    | Multi-step browser flows with auth, async delays, toasts at App root |
| **TOTAL** | **135** | **100%** |              | |

### Distribution vs. Target

| Layer | Actual | Target | Gap | Root Cause |
|-------|--------|--------|-----|-----------|
| Unit | 8.9% | 25% | −16% | `validate()` and `passwordStrength()` are embedded inside components — not currently extractable as pure module functions. See §5 Anti-Patterns. |
| Component | 60.7% | 35% | +26% | 50+ E2E scenarios in scenarios.md were validating static mock data or single-component behavior — all pushed down per decision rule 4. |
| E2E | 30.4% | 40% | −10% | After push-down, only genuine multi-step flows with async delays or cross-component state remain at E2E. |

**Recommended action to fix Unit gap:** Extract `SignIn.validate()` (App.jsx:108–116), `SignUp.validate()` (App.jsx:185–196), `SendMoneyPage.validate()` (App.jsx:403–411), `passwordStrength()` (App.jsx:172–179), and the `TransactionsPage` filter predicate (App.jsx:336–342) into a shared `src/utils/validators.js` module. This would shift approximately 15 tests to Unit, bringing the distribution to ~19% Unit / 50% Component / 31% E2E — closer to the pyramid ideal without changing test logic.

---

## 2. Layer Assignments by Feature

### Auth — Sign In

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-001 | Sign In — Happy Path (Demo User) | **E2E** | Multi-step: fill email + password → click submit → await 1,200ms delay (App.jsx:122–126) → assert `dashboard-page` visible. Async timing and cross-component navigation requires real browser. |
| TC-002 | Sign In — Submit Button Loading State | **E2E** | Must observe `signin-submit` disabled state during 1,200ms async window before navigation completes. Timing-sensitive; Playwright's natural async is the right tool (App.jsx:154). |
| TC-003 | Sign In — Empty Email Field | **Component** | ↓ Pushed down from E2E. No navigation occurs. Render `SignIn`, click submit with empty email, assert `email-error` renders "Email is required". Single component isolation (App.jsx:110). |
| TC-004 | Sign In — Invalid Email Format | **Unit** | Primary: test `validate()` email regex `/\S+@\S+\.\S+/` (App.jsx:111) with input "notanemail" → returns `{email: "Invalid email format"}`. Defense-in-depth: also needs a Component test asserting `email-error` renders. |
| TC-005 | Sign In — Empty Password Field | **Component** | ↓ Pushed down from E2E. No navigation. Render `SignIn`, fill email, leave password empty, submit → assert `password-error` renders "Password is required" (App.jsx:112). |
| TC-006 | Sign In — Password Too Short (Under 6 Chars) | **Unit** | Primary: test `validate()` condition `password.length < 6` (App.jsx:113) with "12345" → returns `{password: "Password must be at least 6 characters"}`. Defense-in-depth: also needs a Component test. |
| TC-007 | Sign In — Wrong Credentials (Valid Format) | **E2E** | Credential check happens inside `setTimeout` at App.jsx:123 after 1,200ms. The `form-error` element is set from the async callback (App.jsx:125). Requires full async browser execution. |
| TC-008 | Sign In — Password Visibility Toggle (Show) | **Component** | Single component state: `showPw` toggles `signin-password` type between "password" and "text" (App.jsx:103, 145). No navigation or async. |
| TC-009 | Sign In — Password Visibility Toggle (Hide Again) | **Component** | Same `showPw` toggle — second click reverts type to "password". Pure state toggle within `SignIn` (App.jsx:103). |
| TC-010 | Sign In — Navigate to Sign Up | **E2E** | `goto-signup` triggers `setPage("signup")` in App root (App.jsx:718), swapping `SignIn` → `SignUp` components. Cross-component page machine; multi-component flow. |
| TC-011 | Sign In — Both Fields Empty (Multiple Errors) | **Component** | ↓ Pushed down from E2E. No navigation. Render `SignIn`, submit with both fields empty → `validate()` collects both errors simultaneously (App.jsx:109–115) → both error elements render. |

### Auth — Sign Up

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-012 | Sign Up — Happy Path (New User) | **E2E** | Multi-step: navigate to signup → fill 4 fields → check terms → submit → await 1,200ms (App.jsx:201–204) → assert dashboard with new user balance $1,000.00. |
| TC-013 | Sign Up — Loading State During Submit | **E2E** | Must observe `signup-submit` disabled + "Creating account..." text during 1,200ms async window. Same timing pattern as TC-002 (App.jsx:247). |
| TC-014 | Sign Up — Blank Name Field | **Component** | ↓ Pushed down from E2E. Render `SignUp` directly (bypassing navigation), submit with blank name → `name-error` renders "Name is required". Single component (App.jsx:187). |
| TC-015 | Sign Up — Whitespace-Only Name | **Unit** | Test `validate()` condition `!form.name.trim()` (App.jsx:187) with input `"   "` → returns `{name: "Name is required"}`. Pure string logic, no DOM. |
| TC-016 | Sign Up — Invalid Email Format | **Unit** | Test `validate()` email regex on "invalidemail" → returns `{email: "Invalid email format"}`. Same regex as SignIn (App.jsx:189). |
| TC-017 | Sign Up — Password Under 8 Characters | **Unit** | Test `validate()` condition `form.password.length < 8` (App.jsx:191) with "Ab1@567" (7 chars) → returns `{password: "At least 8 characters required"}`. |
| TC-018 | Sign Up — Passwords Do Not Match | **Unit** | Test `validate()` condition `form.password !== form.confirmPassword` (App.jsx:192) with mismatched passwords → returns `{confirmPassword: "Passwords don't match"}`. |
| TC-019 | Sign Up — Terms Not Checked | **Component** | ↓ Pushed down from E2E. Render `SignUp`, fill all fields validly, leave `agree-terms` unchecked, submit → `terms-error` renders "You must agree to terms" (App.jsx:193). |
| TC-020 | Sign Up — Password Strength Bar Hidden | **Component** | `password-strength` renders only when `form.password` is truthy (App.jsx:230: `{form.password && ...}`). Render `SignUp`, assert bar absent initially. |
| TC-021 | Sign Up — Password Strength Score 1 (Weak) | **Component** | Render `SignUp`, type "abcdefgh" in `signup-password`. Assert `passwordStrength()` returns 1, one segment filled, label "Weak", color #ef4444 (App.jsx:172–183). |
| TC-022 | Sign Up — Password Strength Score 2 (Fair) | **Component** | Type "Abcdefgh" → score 2, "Fair", #f59e0b. Verifies uppercase criterion (App.jsx:175). |
| TC-023 | Sign Up — Password Strength Score 3 (Good) | **Component** | Type "Abcdefg1" → score 3, "Good", #3b82f6. Verifies number criterion (App.jsx:176). |
| TC-024 | Sign Up — Password Strength Score 4 (Strong) | **Component** | Type "Abcdefg1!" → score 4, "Strong", #10b981. Verifies special char criterion (App.jsx:177). |
| TC-025 | Sign Up — Navigate Back to Sign In | **E2E** | `goto-signin` triggers `setPage("signin")` in App root (App.jsx:249/719), swapping components. Cross-component page machine. |

### Navigation

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-026 | Navigation — Dashboard Sidebar Item | **E2E** | Requires authenticated state (login first), then click `nav-dashboard` → `setActivePage("dashboard")` (App.jsx:698) → `dashboard-page` renders. Multi-step. |
| TC-027 | Navigation — Transactions Sidebar Item | **E2E** | Same pattern — click `nav-transactions` → `transactions-page` visible, `nav-transactions` has `.active` class. Requires prior auth. |
| TC-028 | Navigation — Send Money Sidebar Item | **E2E** | Auth + navigate to send-money-page. Multi-step. |
| TC-029 | Navigation — Cards Sidebar Item | **E2E** | Auth + navigate to cards-page. Multi-step. |
| TC-030 | Navigation — Budget Sidebar Item | **E2E** | Auth + navigate to budget-page. Multi-step. |
| TC-031 | Navigation — Settings Sidebar Item | **E2E** | Auth + navigate to settings-page. Multi-step. |
| TC-032 | Navigation — Logout Clears Auth State | **E2E** | `handleLogout` sets `user=null`, `page="signin"` (App.jsx:696). Cross-component state teardown → auth layout disappears, signin-page appears. |
| TC-033 | Navigation — Cannot Return to App After Logout | **E2E** | Requires browser back-button interaction after logout. No React state is persisted (user-flows.md § Known Limitations). Browser-level behavior. |
| TC-034 | Navigation — Active Nav Item Has Active Class | **Component** | Render Sidebar (or App) with varying `activePage` prop values. Assert exactly one nav item has `.nav-item.active` class at a time (App.jsx:730 ternary). No async, no auth needed — prop-driven CSS class. |
| TC-035 | Navigation — Dashboard Quick Action: Send Money | **E2E** | Requires auth + being on Dashboard. `quick-send` calls `navigateTo("send")` (App.jsx:310). Cross-component routing. |
| TC-036 | Navigation — Dashboard Quick Action: My Cards | **E2E** | Same pattern — `quick-cards` → cards-page (App.jsx:313). |
| TC-037 | Navigation — Dashboard Quick Action: Budget | **E2E** | Same pattern — `quick-budget` → budget-page (App.jsx:316). |
| TC-038 | Navigation — View All Transactions From Dashboard | **E2E** | `view-all-txn` calls `navigateTo("transactions")` (App.jsx:287). Requires auth + dashboard context. |

### Dashboard

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-039 | Dashboard — Total Balance Stat Card | **Component** | ↓ Pushed down from E2E. Render `<DashboardPage user={mockUser} />` with `balance: 24850.75`. Assert `stat-balance` text contains "$24,850.75" (App.jsx:263). No auth or navigation needed. |
| TC-040 | Dashboard — Income Stat Card | **Component** | ↓ Static hardcoded string "$6,432" (App.jsx:269). Render `DashboardPage`, assert `stat-income` text. No dynamic logic. |
| TC-041 | Dashboard — Expenses Stat Card | **Component** | ↓ Static string "$2,180" (App.jsx:273). Render `DashboardPage`, assert `stat-expenses`. |
| TC-042 | Dashboard — Savings Stat Card | **Component** | ↓ Static string "$4,252" (App.jsx:277). Render `DashboardPage`, assert `stat-savings`. |
| TC-043 | Dashboard — Recent Transactions Shows Exactly 5 Rows | **Component** | ↓ `MOCK_TRANSACTIONS.slice(0, 5)` at App.jsx:257. Render `DashboardPage`, count `<tr>` elements in `recent-transactions-table` tbody. Pure render assertion. |
| TC-044 | Dashboard — Recent Transactions Correct Row Content | **Component** | ↓ Render `DashboardPage`, assert `txn-row-TXN-001` cell text matches MOCK_TRANSACTIONS[0] fields. Static data check. |
| TC-045 | Dashboard — All Three Quick Action Buttons Present | **Component** | Render `DashboardPage` with `navigateTo` mock prop. Assert `quick-send`, `quick-cards`, `quick-budget` all present in DOM (App.jsx:309–319). |

### Transactions

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-046 | Transactions — All 12 Records Render | **Component** | ↓ Render `TransactionsPage` directly — it uses `MOCK_TRANSACTIONS` directly (no props). Assert tbody has 12 `<tr>` rows. No auth, no navigation. |
| TC-047 | Transactions — Search by Description (Case-Insensitive) | **Component** | ↓ Render `TransactionsPage`, change `txn-search` value to "netflix". Filter predicate runs (App.jsx:337). Assert 1 row visible. Component interaction. |
| TC-048 | Transactions — Search by Description (Uppercase) | **Component** | ↓ Same filter interaction with "AMAZON" input. Case-insensitive via `.toLowerCase()` (App.jsx:337). |
| TC-049 | Transactions — Search by Transaction ID | **Component** | ↓ Search "TXN-006" matches `t.id.toLowerCase().includes(...)` (App.jsx:337). Assert TXN-006 row visible. |
| TC-050 | Transactions — Search With No Matches | **Component** | ↓ Input "zzznotfound" → `filtered.length === 0` → `no-results` div renders (App.jsx:367–369). Component empty-state test. |
| TC-051 | Transactions — Filter by Type: Credit (3 Results) | **Component** | ↓ Change `filter-type` to "credit" → filter condition `t.type !== typeFilter` (App.jsx:338) → 3 rows. |
| TC-052 | Transactions — Filter by Type: Debit (9 Results) | **Component** | ↓ Change `filter-type` to "debit" → 9 rows. |
| TC-053 | Transactions — Filter by Status: Pending (1 Result) | **Component** | ↓ Change `filter-status` to "pending" → `t.status !== statusFilter` (App.jsx:339) → TXN-006 only. |
| TC-054 | Transactions — Filter by Status: Completed (11 Results) | **Component** | ↓ Change `filter-status` to "completed" → 11 rows. |
| TC-055 | Transactions — Filter by Category: Entertainment | **Component** | ↓ Change `filter-category` to "Entertainment" → 2 rows (TXN-001, TXN-012). |
| TC-056 | Transactions — Filter by Category: Income | **Component** | ↓ Change `filter-category` to "Income" → 2 rows (TXN-002, TXN-007). |
| TC-057 | Transactions — Combined Filter: Credit + Income | **Component** | ↓ Change both `filter-type` (credit) and `filter-category` (Income). All conditions are ANDed (App.jsx:336–342). 2 rows. |
| TC-058 | Transactions — Combined Filter: Pending + Debit | **Component** | ↓ Set `filter-status` pending + `filter-type` debit → 1 row. AND logic. |
| TC-059 | Transactions — Combined Filter Returns Empty State | **Component** | ↓ Set pending + credit → 0 results → `no-results` renders. |
| TC-060 | Transactions — Credit Amount Has Green + Prefix | **Component** | Assert `txn-amount.credit` class and "+" prefix on TXN-002 row (App.jsx:381). Render `TransactionsPage`. |
| TC-061 | Transactions — Debit Amount Has Red Minus Prefix | **Component** | Assert `txn-amount.debit` class and "-" prefix on TXN-001 row (App.jsx:381). |
| TC-062 | Transactions — Pending Status Badge Styling | **Component** | Assert `txn-status.pending` class on TXN-006 row (App.jsx:381). |
| TC-063 | Transactions — Completed Status Badge Styling | **Component** | Assert `txn-status.completed` class on TXN-001 row (App.jsx:381). |
| TC-064 | Transactions — Clearing Search Restores All Results | **Component** | ↓ Fill `txn-search` → clear → all 12 rows reappear. Filter re-evaluates on input change (App.jsx:337). |

### Send Money

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-065 | Send Money — Happy Path via Contact Chip (With Note) | **E2E** | Full flow: login → navigate → select chip → fill amount → fill note → submit → modal → confirm → await 1,500ms (App.jsx:420–425) → toast at App root → form reset. Async + multi-component. |
| TC-066 | Send Money — Happy Path via Manual Email (No Note) | **E2E** | Same multi-step flow with manual email path. 1,500ms delay + App-root toast. |
| TC-067 | Send Money — Selecting Contact Chip Clears Manual Email | **Component** | Click chip sets `selected = c.id` AND `setCustomEmail("")` (App.jsx:437). Single component state assertion. No navigation. |
| TC-068 | Send Money — Typing Email Deselects Contact Chip | **Component** | Typing in `recipient-email` calls `setSelected(null)` (App.jsx:444). Assert chip loses `.selected` class. Single component. |
| TC-069 | Send Money — No Recipient Selected | **Component** | ↓ Render `<SendMoneyPage user={mockUser} showToast={mockFn} />`. Click `send-submit` with no contact/email → `validate()` (App.jsx:405) → `recipient-error` renders. No modal, no async. |
| TC-070 | Send Money — Invalid Manual Email Format | **Component** | ↓ Fill `recipient-email` with "notanemail", fill amount → `validate()` (App.jsx:406) → `recipient-error` "Invalid email". |
| TC-071 | Send Money — Amount of Zero | **Component** | ↓ Select contact, fill amount "0" → `parseFloat(amount) <= 0` (App.jsx:407) → `amount-error` "Enter a valid amount". |
| TC-072 | Send Money — Amount Exceeds Balance | **Component** | ↓ Select contact, fill amount "99999" → `parseFloat(amount) > user.balance` (App.jsx:408) → `amount-error` "Insufficient balance". No modal. Defense-in-depth: also Unit test for the condition, E2E smoke. |
| TC-073 | Send Money — Amount Exactly Equal to Balance (Boundary) | **Unit** | Test `validate()` condition `parseFloat("24850.75") > 24850.75` → false (App.jsx:408). Equal-to-balance passes. Pure math, no DOM. |
| TC-074 | Send Money — Cancel via Cancel Button (Form Preserved) | **Component** | ↓ Render `SendMoneyPage`, fill valid data, submit → `showConfirm=true` (synchronous, no delay), modal renders → click `modal-cancel` → `setShowConfirm(false)` (App.jsx:472) → assert form fields unchanged. Modal is inline in SendMoneyPage. |
| TC-075 | Send Money — Cancel via Overlay Click (Form Preserved) | **Component** | ↓ Same as TC-074 — click `modal-overlay` fires `onCancel` (App.jsx:86, `Modal` overlay onClick). Modal is rendered inside `SendMoneyPage`. |
| TC-076 | Send Money — Confirm Loading State During Transfer | **E2E** | `modal-confirm` label changes to "Sending..." during 1,500ms `setSending(true)` window (App.jsx:419–425). Timing-sensitive observation requires browser. |
| TC-077 | Send Money — Balance Not Deducted After Transfer | **E2E** | Known limitation (user-flows.md § Known Limitations). Multi-page flow: send money → navigate to dashboard → verify balance unchanged. P3 document-only test. |
| TC-078 | Send Money — Note Is Fully Optional | **Component** | ↓ Render `SendMoneyPage`, select chip, fill amount, leave note empty, click submit → modal renders with text `"Send $25.00 to David R.?"` without note clause (App.jsx:469). Modal appears synchronously. |

### Cards

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-079 | Cards — Both Cards Render With Correct Details | **Component** | ↓ Render `<CardsPage showToast={mockFn} />`. Assert `card-CARD-001` and `card-CARD-002` present with correct type/last4/expiry from `MOCK_CARDS` (App.jsx:23–26). No auth. |
| TC-080 | Cards — Visa Card Has Dark Background When Active | **Component** | Assert `card-CARD-001` inline style `background: #0f172a`, `opacity: 1` (App.jsx:495: `c.status === "active"` → `c.color`). Render `CardsPage`. |
| TC-081 | Cards — Mastercard Has Purple Background When Active | **Component** | Assert `card-CARD-002` inline style `background: #7c3aed`, `opacity: 1` (App.jsx:495). |
| TC-082 | Cards — Freeze Visa Card — Visual Change | **Component** | ↓ Render `CardsPage`, click `freeze-CARD-001` → `toggleFreeze` (App.jsx:484–488) → `cards` state updates → card inline style becomes `{background: "#374151", opacity: 0.7}` (App.jsx:495–496). Assert style + FROZEN badge. |
| TC-083 | Cards — Freeze Visa Card — Toast Notification | **E2E** | Toast element (`toast-message`) is rendered at App root (App.jsx:756), not inside `CardsPage`. Verifying "Card frozen" text in the actual DOM requires App context. |
| TC-084 | Cards — Freeze Button Label Changes to Unfreeze | **Component** | After `toggleFreeze`, `c.status` becomes "frozen" → button label renders "Unfreeze" (App.jsx:509). State transition in `CardsPage`. |
| TC-085 | Cards — Unfreeze Visa Card — Visual Restored | **E2E** | Requires: auth → navigate → freeze state → unfreeze → assert visual restore + toast ("Card unfrozen"). The toast assertion requires App root context. Multi-step. |
| TC-086 | Cards — Freeze Mastercard Independently | **E2E** | Verify independent card state after freezing one card — CARD-001 unchanged, CARD-002 frozen. Includes toast check at App root. Multi-step post-auth. |
| TC-087 | Cards — Lock Card Opens Danger Modal | **Component** | ↓ Render `CardsPage`, click `lock-CARD-001` → `setShowLockModal("CARD-001")` (App.jsx:511) → `Modal` renders inline within `CardsPage` (App.jsx:539–548). Assert modal visible with correct text. |
| TC-088 | Cards — Lock Card Modal Has Danger Styling | **Component** | `Modal` rendered with `danger={true}` (App.jsx:544) → `modal-confirm` gets class `btn-danger` (App.jsx:92). Render `CardsPage`, trigger lock modal, assert button class. |
| TC-089 | Cards — Lock Card Confirm Shows Error Toast | **E2E** | `onConfirm` calls `showToast("Card locked permanently", "error")` (App.jsx:545). Toast rendered at App root. Requires App context. |
| TC-090 | Cards — Lock Card Does Not Change Card UI Status | **E2E** | Known limitation (user-flows.md § Known Limitations). P3. Confirm lock → card still appears active visually. Multi-step. |
| TC-091 | Cards — Lock Card Cancel Closes Modal With No Toast | **Component** | ↓ Render `CardsPage`, trigger lock modal, click `modal-cancel` → `setShowLockModal(null)` (App.jsx:546) → modal disappears. Assert `showToast` mock not called. No async. |
| TC-092 | Cards — Card Limits Table Renders Correctly | **Component** | ↓ Render `CardsPage`, assert `card-limits-table` rows show correct limit/used/remaining values. `$347.00` used today is hardcoded (App.jsx:531). Static data assertion. |

### Budget

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-093 | Budget — Total Budget Stat Card | **Component** | ↓ Render `BudgetPage`. `totalBudget = BUDGET_DATA.reduce((s, b) => s + b.budget, 0)` = $1,400 (App.jsx:555). Assert `total-budget` text. No auth. |
| TC-094 | Budget — Total Spent Stat Card | **Component** | ↓ `totalSpent = BUDGET_DATA.reduce((s, b) => s + b.spent, 0)` = 930.95 (App.jsx:556). Assert `total-spent` text. |
| TC-095 | Budget — Remaining Budget Stat Card | **Component** | ↓ `$(totalBudget - totalSpent).toFixed(2)` = "$469.05", `color: "#10b981"` (App.jsx:571). Assert value and green inline color on `remaining-budget`. |
| TC-096 | Budget — All Six Category Cards Visible | **Component** | ↓ Render `BudgetPage`, assert all 6 `budget-*` testid elements present. `BUDGET_DATA.map(...)` (App.jsx:576). |
| TC-097 | Budget — Groceries Category Values | **Component** | ↓ Assert `budget-groceries` shows "$287.43 / $400" and `pct = Math.min((287.43/400)*100, 100)` = 71.86% → "72% used". Progress bar fill uses category color `#10b981` (pct < 90) (App.jsx:588). |
| TC-098 | Budget — Shopping Near-Limit (88.3%, NOT Red) | **Component** | Assert `budget-shopping` bar fill is `#ef4444` (Shopping's own category color, not the warning color) at 88.3%. `pct > 90` is false at 88.3 → uses `b.color = "#ef4444"` (App.jsx:588). Important edge: same color, different reason. |
| TC-099 | Budget — Progress Bar Turns Red at ≥ 90% | **Unit** | Test `pct > 90 ? "#ef4444" : b.color` (App.jsx:588) with `pct = 90.0` → uses `b.color` (false). With `pct = 90.1` → `"#ef4444"`. Pure ternary logic, no DOM. |
| TC-100 | Budget — Progress Bar Capped at 100% When Over Budget | **Unit** | Test `Math.min((spent / budget) * 100, 100)` (App.jsx:577) with `spent=500, budget=400` → `Math.min(125, 100)` = 100. Pure math, no DOM. |

### Settings

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-101 | Settings — Profile Fields Pre-Filled With User Data | **Component** | ↓ Render `<SettingsPage user={mockUser} showToast={mockFn} />`. Assert `settings-name` value = "Alex Morgan", etc. (App.jsx:602). No auth or navigation. |
| TC-102 | Settings — Save Profile Shows Success Toast | **E2E** | `handleProfileSave` calls `showToast("Profile updated", "success")` (App.jsx:610). Toast rendered at App root (App.jsx:756). Requires App context to assert `toast-message` element. |
| TC-103 | Settings — 2FA Toggle Default Is ON | **Component** | Initial `toggles.twoFactor = true` (App.jsx:601). Render `SettingsPage`, assert `toggle-twoFactor` has class `.on` without any interaction. |
| TC-104 | Settings — Toggle 2FA Off | **E2E** | Click `toggle-twoFactor` → `showToast("two Factor disabled", "success")` (App.jsx:607). Toast at App root requires App context. |
| TC-105 | Settings — Toggle 2FA Back On | **E2E** | Second click → `showToast("two Factor enabled", "success")`. Toast at App root. |
| TC-106 | Settings — Biometric Toggle Default Is OFF | **Component** | Initial `toggles.biometric = false` (App.jsx:601). Assert `toggle-biometric` does NOT have `.on` class. |
| TC-107 | Settings — Toggle Biometric On | **E2E** | Click `toggle-biometric` → `showToast("biometric enabled", "success")`. Toast at App root. |
| TC-108 | Settings — Push Notifications Default Is ON | **Component** | Initial `toggles.notifications = true` (App.jsx:601). Assert `toggle-notifications` has `.on` class. |
| TC-109 | Settings — Email Alerts Default Is ON | **Component** | Initial `toggles.emailAlerts = true` (App.jsx:601). Assert `toggle-emailAlerts` has `.on` class. |
| TC-110 | Settings — SMS Alerts Default Is OFF | **Component** | Initial `toggles.smsAlerts = false` (App.jsx:601). Assert `toggle-smsAlerts` does NOT have `.on` class. |
| TC-111 | Settings — Toggle SMS Alerts On | **E2E** | Click → `showToast("sms Alerts enabled", "success")`. Toast at App root. |
| TC-112 | Settings — Delete Account Opens Danger Modal | **Component** | ↓ Render `SettingsPage`, click `delete-account` → `setShowDeleteModal(true)` (App.jsx:665) → `Modal` renders inline (App.jsx:669–678). Assert modal visible + exact text + `btn-danger` class. |
| TC-113 | Settings — Delete Account Confirm Shows Error Toast | **E2E** | `onConfirm` calls `showToast("Account deletion requested", "error")` (App.jsx:675). Toast at App root. |
| TC-114 | Settings — Delete Account Does Not Log User Out | **E2E** | Known limitation (user-flows.md). P3. Confirm deletion → user stays on settings page, `app-layout` still visible. Multi-step. |
| TC-115 | Settings — Delete Account Cancel Closes Modal | **Component** | ↓ Render `SettingsPage`, open delete modal, click `modal-cancel` → `setShowDeleteModal(false)` (App.jsx:676) → modal disappears. Assert `showToast` mock not called. |
| TC-116 | Settings — Change Password Button Is Present (No-Op) | **Component** | Render `SettingsPage`, assert `change-password` button present. Click it → no toast, no modal, no navigation (App.jsx:643). Existence + harmlessness assertion. |

### Toast & Modal (Cross-Cutting)

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-117 | Toast — Success Type Has Green Style and Check Icon | **Component** | Render `<Toast message="Test" type="success" onClose={mockFn} />`. Assert green class, `<Icons.Check />` renders (App.jsx:74–81). |
| TC-118 | Toast — Error Type Has Red Style and X Icon | **Component** | Render `<Toast message="Test" type="error" onClose={mockFn} />`. Assert red class, `<Icons.X />` renders (App.jsx:78: `type === "success"` ? Check : X). |
| TC-119 | Toast — Auto-Dismisses After 3 Seconds | **Component** | Render `Toast`, use jest fake timers, advance 3,000ms → `onClose` called (App.jsx:75: `setTimeout(onClose, 3000)`). Assert component unmounts/closes. |
| TC-120 | Toast — New Toast Overwrites Previous Toast | **Component** | Render App or a state wrapper. Call `showToast` twice rapidly → `setToast({ message, type })` overwrites state (App.jsx:690–692). Assert only one `toast-message` element, second message shown. |
| TC-121 | Modal — Clicking Overlay Dismisses Modal | **Component** | Render `<Modal ... onCancel={mockFn} />`. Click `modal-overlay` → `onClick={onCancel}` fires (App.jsx:86). Assert `mockFn` called. |
| TC-122 | Modal — Clicking Inside Modal Box Does Not Dismiss | **Component** | Click `modal` (inner div) → `e.stopPropagation()` (App.jsx:87) prevents overlay's onClick. Assert `onCancel` NOT called. |
| TC-123 | Modal — Non-Danger Modal Has Blue Confirm Button | **Component** | Render `<Modal danger={false} ... />`. Assert `modal-confirm` has class `btn-primary` (App.jsx:92: `danger ? "btn-danger" : "btn-primary"`). |

### Security & Edge Cases

| TC ID | Title | Assigned Layer | Rationale |
|-------|-------|---------------|-----------|
| TC-124 | Security — XSS-Like Input in Send Money Note | **E2E** | Full flow: login → navigate → fill `<script>alert("xss")</script>` in note → submit → observe modal content rendered as plain text. Multi-step + security verification in real browser. |
| TC-125 | Security — XSS-Like Input in Transactions Search | **E2E** | Login → navigate to transactions → fill XSS payload in `txn-search` → verify no script executes and app remains stable. Requires real browser environment. |
| TC-126 | Security — Sign In With SQL Injection Pattern | **Unit** | Test `validate()` email regex (App.jsx:111) with `"' OR 1=1 --"` → fails `/\S+@\S+\.\S+/` → returns `{email: "Invalid email format"}`. Pure regex evaluation, no DOM. |
| TC-127 | Edge Case — Very Large Amount in Send Money | **Component** | ↓ Render `SendMoneyPage` with user `{balance: 24850.75}`, select contact, enter "999999999999" → `parseFloat(amount) > user.balance` (App.jsx:408) → `amount-error` "Insufficient balance". |
| TC-128 | Edge Case — Negative Amount in Send Money | **Unit** | Test `validate()` condition `parseFloat("-100") <= 0` → true (App.jsx:407) → returns `{amount: "Enter a valid amount"}`. Pure math, no DOM. |
| TC-129 | Edge Case — State Resets After Page Refresh | **E2E** | Browser-level page refresh (Playwright `page.reload()`) → all React state lost → must re-login, cards active, toggles at defaults. Only testable in real browser. |
| TC-130 | Edge Case — Sending to All Four Contacts Works | **E2E** | Multi-contact send flow — select each of C-001 through C-004 across multiple iterations. Full 1,500ms delay × contacts. Multi-step. |
| TC-131 | Edge Case — Decimal Amount in Send Money | **Component** | ↓ Render `SendMoneyPage`, select contact, enter "24.99" → modal shows `"Send $24.99 to Sarah K.?"` via `parseFloat(amount).toFixed(2)` (App.jsx:469). Synchronous, no async delay needed for assertion. |
| TC-132 | Edge Case — Budget Page Percentage Display Rounding | **Unit** | Test `(287.43/400 * 100).toFixed(0)` (App.jsx:590) → "72" (71.8575 rounds up). Pure math expression, no DOM. |
| TC-133 | Security — No-Op Buttons Are Present But Harmless | **E2E** | Tests span multiple pages: Cards (`details-CARD-001`), global header (`global-search`, `notifications-btn`), Sign In (`forgot-password`), Settings (`change-password`). Cross-page; requires login + navigation to each page. |
| TC-134 | Edge Case — Rapid Toggle Clicks on Settings | **Component** | Render `SettingsPage`, click `toggle-twoFactor` 4 times rapidly. Assert final state reflects even number of clicks (back to `.on`). React batching test (App.jsx:605). No toast assertion. |
| TC-135 | Security — Sign In Credentials Not Exposed in DOM | **E2E** | Requires DevTools-level inspection of input `type` attribute in a real browser. Playwright's `getAttribute("type")` on `signin-password` = "password" when `showPw=false` (App.jsx:145). |

---

## 3. Contested Assignments

These TCs had non-obvious layer decisions or changed from the `scenarios.md` suggestion:

| TC ID | Suggested in scenarios.md | Final Assignment | Reason | Source |
|-------|--------------------------|-----------------|--------|--------|
| TC-003 | E2E | **Component** | No navigation occurs; error renders within `SignIn` component after submit. Push-down rule 4. | App.jsx:110, 140 |
| TC-004 | Unit + E2E | **Unit** (primary) | The regex logic is pure. E2E smoke is covered by TC-007. Defense-in-depth: add Component test for error element. | App.jsx:111 |
| TC-005 | E2E | **Component** | No navigation. `password-error` renders within `SignIn`. Push-down rule 4. | App.jsx:112, 148 |
| TC-006 | Unit + E2E | **Unit** (primary) | Length check is pure. E2E smoke covered by TC-001/TC-007. Defense-in-depth: add Component. | App.jsx:113 |
| TC-011 | E2E | **Component** | Both errors rendered by `validate()` within single `SignIn` component. No navigation. Push-down. | App.jsx:109–115 |
| TC-014 | E2E | **Component** | Render `SignUp` directly — bypasses page navigation entirely. Error appears within single component. | App.jsx:187, 217 |
| TC-019 | E2E | **Component** | Terms check is pure state within `SignUp`. Render directly, assert `terms-error`. Push-down. | App.jsx:193, 245 |
| TC-034 | Component | **Component** ✓ | Confirmed. CSS class `.nav-item.active` is prop-driven (`activePage === n.id`, App.jsx:730). Render Sidebar with different `activePage` values — no auth or async needed. | App.jsx:730 |
| TC-039–044 | E2E | **Component** | Static data assertions on `DashboardPage` props. Can render with mock user, no auth. Major push-down. | App.jsx:256–325 |
| TC-046–059, TC-064 | E2E | **Component** | All filter/search interactions are pure state within `TransactionsPage` (no props, no auth, no async). Major push-down from ice-cream-cone pattern. | App.jsx:328–391 |
| TC-069–072, TC-074, TC-075, TC-078 | E2E | **Component** | Validation and synchronous modal flow within `SendMoneyPage`. Modal appears/closes synchronously — no 1,500ms delay until `modal-confirm` is clicked. Push-down. | App.jsx:394–476 |
| TC-079, TC-092 | E2E | **Component** | Static card data and table render from MOCK_CARDS. Render `CardsPage` directly. | App.jsx:480–551 |
| TC-082 | E2E | **Component** | `toggleFreeze` updates local state, card inline styles change — no App-root involvement until `showToast` is called. Test visual change, mock `showToast`. | App.jsx:484–496 |
| TC-083, TC-085, TC-086, TC-089 | E2E | **E2E** ✓ | Kept E2E. Toast element is rendered at App root (App.jsx:756), not inside CardsPage. Verifying `toast-message` DOM presence requires App context. | App.jsx:756 |
| TC-087, TC-091 | E2E | **Component** | Lock card modal renders INSIDE `CardsPage` (App.jsx:539–548). Cancel has no App-root side effects. Push-down. | App.jsx:539–548 |
| TC-093–097, TC-101 | E2E | **Component** | Static renders from BUDGET_DATA / user props. Push-down from static-data-at-E2E anti-pattern. | App.jsx:554–602 |
| TC-112, TC-115 | E2E | **Component** | Delete modal renders INSIDE `SettingsPage` (App.jsx:669–678). Cancel has no App-root side effects. | App.jsx:669–678 |
| TC-127, TC-131 | E2E | **Component** | TC-127: validation result only — no modal needed. TC-131: modal appears synchronously (no async delay before assertion). Push-down. | App.jsx:407–408, 469 |

---

## 4. Defense-in-Depth Assignments

These business rules are critical enough to require coverage at multiple layers. The table shows which TC(s) cover each layer.

| Business Rule | Unit TC | Component TC | E2E TC |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sign-in email format regex | TC-004 ✓ | **MISSING** — add Component test: render `SignIn`, enter "notanemail", assert `email-error` | TC-007 (wrong creds smoke) |
| Sign-in password min 6 chars | TC-006 ✓ | **MISSING** — add Component test: render `SignIn`, enter 5-char password, assert `password-error` | TC-001 (happy path excludes short pw) |
| Sign-up password min 8 chars | TC-017 ✓ | **MISSING** — add Component test: render `SignUp`, enter 7-char password, assert `password-error` | TC-012 (happy path indirectly) |
| Amount > balance → insufficient | TC-073 (boundary: equal passes) ✓ | TC-072 (> balance → error renders) ✓ | TC-127 (very large amount, E2E) ✓ |
| Email format regex (sign-up) | TC-016 ✓ | TC-019 indirectly | TC-012 (happy path smoke) |
| passwordStrength() score | **MISSING** — no Unit TC for `passwordStrength()` function itself | TC-021–024 ✓ | (not needed at E2E) |
| Transaction filter predicate | **MISSING** — no Unit TC for the filter function | TC-047–059 ✓ | TC-057–058 (combined filters, E2E-equivalent via Component) |

### Coverage Gaps to Address

1. **`passwordStrength()` Unit test** — `passwordStrength()` at App.jsx:172–179 is a pure function (no DOM, no React). Add a Unit test:
   ```js
   // unit/password-strength.test.js
   describe('passwordStrength', () => {
     test('returns 0 for empty string', () => expect(passwordStrength('abc')).toBe(0));
     test('returns 1 for length ≥ 8 only', () => expect(passwordStrength('abcdefgh')).toBe(1));
     test('returns 4 for all criteria', () => expect(passwordStrength('Abcdefg1!')).toBe(4));
   });
   ```

2. **Transaction filter predicate Unit test** — The filter at App.jsx:336–342 is pure logic. Add:
   ```js
   // unit/transaction-filter.test.js
   test('search is case-insensitive', () => {
     const result = filterTxns(MOCK_TRANSACTIONS, { search: 'NETFLIX' });
     expect(result).toHaveLength(1);
     expect(result[0].id).toBe('TXN-001');
   });
   ```

3. **Sign-in min-6-chars Component test** — TC-006 covers Unit; add a separate Component test to confirm error element renders in the DOM.

4. **Sign-up min-8-chars Component test** — TC-017 covers Unit; add Component test to confirm `password-error` element renders.

---

## 5. Anti-Patterns Found

| Anti-Pattern | TC IDs Affected | Why It's Wrong | Recommended Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Ice cream cone** (everything at E2E) | TC-003, TC-005, TC-011, TC-014, TC-019, TC-039–044, TC-046–059, TC-064, TC-069–072, TC-074, TC-075, TC-078, TC-079, TC-092–097, TC-101, TC-112, TC-115, TC-127, TC-131 (41 TCs) | Slow, brittle; validation errors and static data renders don't need a browser. This was the largest anti-pattern in the original suite — over 60 of 135 tests assigned E2E. | Apply push-down decision rule. Render components directly in @testing-library/react. All affected TCs reassigned to Component in this strategy. |
| **Static mock data accuracy at E2E** | TC-039–044 (Dashboard stats), TC-046 (all 12 txns), TC-079 (card details), TC-093–097 (budget values) | "Value reads '$24,850.75'" doesn't need a browser. Mock data doesn't change. E2E adds 4-8s per assertion that a Component test covers in <100ms. | Move to Component: render page component with mock data, assert text values. |
| **Validation rules tested only at E2E** | TC-003, TC-005, TC-011 (sign-in), TC-014, TC-019 (sign-up), TC-069–072 (send money) | `validate()` functions are pure. Testing them only via the browser is slow and overkill. Also lacks a fast feedback loop for developers. | Add Unit tests for extracted validate() functions. Keep E2E only as P0 smoke tests (e.g., TC-001, TC-012, TC-065). |
| **No Unit tests for `passwordStrength()`** | TC-021–024 only at Component | `passwordStrength()` at App.jsx:172–179 has no DOM dependency. Testing score=2 by typing in a browser input is 1,000× slower than calling the function directly. | Add `unit/password-strength.test.js` covering all 5 score states (0–4). |
| **No Unit tests for filter predicate** | TC-047–059 only at Component | The filter function at App.jsx:336–342 is a pure predicate. Three lines of logic that could run in <1ms are currently only tested at 100ms+ Component layer. | Extract to `src/utils/filter.js`, add `unit/transaction-filter.test.js`. |
| **Known limitations silently skipped** | TC-077 (balance not deducted), TC-090 (lock card no UI update), TC-114 (delete no logout) | These were assigned as normal test cases but the expected behavior IS the limitation. Calling them failures would be incorrect. | Marked P3 in scenarios.md correctly. Document in `docs/known-limitations.md`. In test code, add a `// KNOWN LIMITATION:` comment. Do NOT `expect.fail()` on these. |
| **No-op button behavior tested at E2E** | TC-133 (multi-page no-op sweep) | Testing "nothing happens when clicked" across 4 no-op buttons spanning 4 pages is fragile in E2E. A button click that produces no side effect is the simplest assertion. | Split into per-page Component tests: render page, click no-op button, assert `showToast` mock not called and no DOM changes. TC-133 as written spans too many pages to be maintainable as a single E2E. Keeping E2E but recommend splitting. |
| **CSS class assertion inside E2E** | No current TCs in scenarios.md do this (UI class assertions were already at Component). | E2E testing `.txn-amount.credit` class would be fragile. | Correctly assigned to Component (TC-060–063, TC-088, TC-103, TC-106–110). No fix needed — correct as-is. |

---

## 6. Test Suite Execution Estimate

```
Unit tests:      12 tests × ~2ms avg    = ~24ms      ≈ <1 second
Component tests: 82 tests × ~100ms avg  = ~8,200ms   ≈ 8 seconds
E2E tests:       41 tests × ~5s avg     = ~205s       ≈ 3.4 minutes
                 (includes 1,200ms login fixture per test + action delays)

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Total (sequential):                      ≈ 3.5 minutes
Total (E2E with 4 Playwright workers):   ≈ 60 seconds
Total (practical CI with parallelism):   ≈ 75 seconds
```

### E2E Timing Breakdown

| Flow | Delay Source | Tests Affected | Per-Test Overhead |
|---|---|---|---|
| Login fixture | 1,200ms simulated delay (App.jsx:122) | All 41 E2E tests | +1.2s |
| Sign-up submit | 1,200ms simulated delay (App.jsx:201) | TC-012, TC-013 | +1.2s |
| Send money confirm | 1,500ms simulated delay (App.jsx:420) | TC-065, TC-066, TC-076, TC-130 | +1.5s |
| Toast auto-dismiss | 3,000ms (App.jsx:75) | Do NOT wait for dismiss — use `waitFor` on next action instead | 0s if done correctly |

**Critical:** Never use `page.waitForTimeout(3000)` to wait for toast dismissal. Use `await page.waitForSelector('[data-testid="toast-message"]', { state: 'visible' })` to confirm toast appeared, then proceed — don't block on dismissal.

---

## 7. Recommended Test File Structure

```
tests/
├── unit/
│   ├── auth-validate-signin.test.js    — SignIn validate(): email regex, password min-6, required fields
│   ├── auth-validate-signup.test.js    — SignUp validate(): trim, email, min-8, match, terms
│   ├── send-validate.test.js           — SendMoneyPage validate(): recipient, amount ≤ 0, > balance, boundary
│   ├── password-strength.test.js       — passwordStrength(): scores 0–4, criteria combinations
│   ├── transaction-filter.test.js      — filter predicate: search, type, status, category, AND logic, empty state
│   └── budget-calc.test.js             — pct > 90 threshold, Math.min cap at 100%
│
├── component/
│   ├── signin.test.jsx                 — empty fields, format errors, both-errors, toggle password visibility
│   ├── signup.test.jsx                 — blank name, terms unchecked, strength bar 0–4, password confirm
│   ├── dashboard.test.jsx              — stat cards, 5-row slice, quick action buttons present
│   ├── transactions.test.jsx           — search, filters, combined, CSS classes, empty state, clear
│   ├── send-money.test.jsx             — contact/email exclusivity, validation errors, modal text, cancel, overlay
│   ├── cards.test.jsx                  — render details, active styles, freeze visual, freeze label, lock modal
│   ├── budget.test.jsx                 — totals, 6 categories, per-category values, shopping color edge case
│   ├── settings.test.jsx               — pre-fill, toggle defaults, delete modal, cancel, change-password no-op
│   ├── toast.test.jsx                  — success/error styles, 3s auto-dismiss (fake timers), overwrite
│   ├── modal.test.jsx                  — overlay dismiss, stopPropagation, danger vs primary class
│   └── nav.test.jsx                    — active class follows activePage prop
│
└── e2e/
    ├── auth.spec.js                    — TC-001 happy path, TC-002 loading, TC-007 wrong creds, TC-010 nav, TC-012–013 signup, TC-025 back to signin
    ├── navigation.spec.js              — TC-026–033 all sidebar items, logout, TC-034 active class, TC-035–038 quick actions
    ├── send-money.spec.js              — TC-065–066 happy paths, TC-076 loading state, TC-077 balance not deducted (P3)
    ├── cards.spec.js                   — TC-083 freeze toast, TC-085 unfreeze, TC-086 independent state, TC-089 lock toast, TC-090 (P3)
    ├── settings.spec.js                — TC-102 save profile, TC-104–107 toggle toasts, TC-111, TC-113–114 (P3)
    ├── security.spec.js                — TC-124–125 XSS, TC-129 state reset, TC-130 all contacts, TC-133 no-ops, TC-135 password hidden
    └── known-limitations.spec.js       — TC-077, TC-090, TC-114 (all P3 — document but do not fail)
```

---

## Notes on Known Limitations

Per `user-flows.md § Known Limitations`, the following behaviors are intentional and must NOT be auto-failed:

| TC | Behavior | Recommended Handling |
|---|---|---|
| TC-077 | Balance not deducted after transfer | Assert `stat-balance` STILL equals original — this IS the pass condition. |
| TC-090 | Lock card does not update card UI status | Assert card STILL appears active — this IS the pass condition. |
| TC-114 | Delete account does not log user out | Assert user STILL on settings page — this IS the pass condition. |
| TC-133 | No-op buttons produce no effect | Assert nothing changes — this IS the pass condition. |

All four are P3 priority. Consider tagging them `@known-limitation` to exclude from CI blocking gates while keeping them in the suite for regression detection if behavior ever changes.
